primaryStudies.citation: Brzoska T, Luger TA, Maaser C, Abels C, Bohm M. Alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone and related tripeptides: biochemistry, antiinflammatory and protective effects in vitro and in vivo, and future perspectives for the treatment of immune-mediated inflammatory diseases. Endocr Rev. 2008;29(5):581-602.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review establishing KPV as the minimal anti-inflammatory sequence of alpha-MSH; retains immunomodulatory activity without melanotropic effects

primaryStudies.citation: Luger TA, Scholzen TE, Brzoska T, Bohm M. New insights into the functions of alpha-MSH and related peptides in the immune system. Ann N Y Acad Sci. 2003;994:133-140.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: KPV and alpha-MSH share anti-inflammatory properties; both inhibit NF-kB activation and reduce inflammatory cytokine production

primaryStudies.citation: Kannengiesser K, Maaser C, Heidemann J, et al. Melanocortin-derived tripeptide KPV has anti-inflammatory potential in murine models of inflammatory bowel disease. Inflamm Bowel Dis. 2008;14(3):324-331.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Oral KPV reduced colitis severity, decreased inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6), prevented weight loss; effective at microgram doses

primaryStudies.citation: Dalmasso G, Charrier-Hisamuddin L, Nguyen HT, et al. PepT1-mediated tripeptide KPV uptake reduces intestinal inflammation. Gastroenterology. 2008;134(1):166-178.
primaryStudies.population: Colonic epithelial cells, mouse colitis models
primaryStudies.keyFindings: KPV is transported into cells via PepT1 transporter; inhibits NF-kB and MAPK pathways; reduces colitis when administered orally

primaryStudies.citation: Laroui H, Dalmasso G, Nguyen HT, et al. Drug-loaded nanoparticles targeted to the colon with polysaccharide hydrogel reduce colitis in a mouse model. Gastroenterology. 2010;138(3):843-853.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: KPV-loaded nanoparticles showed enhanced colitis reduction; targeted delivery improved efficacy vs free KPV

primaryStudies.citation: Capsoni F, Ongari AM, Colombo G, et al. The synthetic melanocortin (CKPV)2 exerts broad anti-inflammatory effects in human neutrophils. Peptides. 2007;28(10):2016-2022.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: CKPV dimer inhibited neutrophil chemotaxis, superoxide production, and IL-8 release; anti-inflammatory at nanomolar concentrations

primaryStudies.citation: Catania A, Gatti S, Colombo G, Lipton JM. Alpha-melanocyte stimulating hormone in modulation of host reactions. Endocr Rev. 2004;25(1):159-167.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Review of alpha-MSH and derived peptides including KPV; established mechanisms of action in immune modulation

primaryStudies.citation: Colombo G, Buffa R, Bardella MT, et al. Anti-inflammatory effects of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone in celiac intestinal mucosa. Neuroimmunomodulation. 2002;10(4):208-216.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Alpha-MSH reduced inflammatory markers in celiac disease tissue; KPV sequence responsible for activity

additionalStudies.citation: Getting SJ, Schioth HB, Perretti M. Dissection of the anti-inflammatory effect of the core and C-terminal (KPV) alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone peptides. J Pharmacol Exp Ther. 2003;306(2):631-637.
additionalStudies.keyFindings: KPV retained anti-inflammatory activity independent of melanocortin receptors; suggests alternative mechanism

additionalStudies.citation: Ichiyama T, Zhao H, Catania A, et al. Alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone inhibits NF-kB activation and IkBa degradation in human glioma cells and in experimental brain inflammation. Exp Neurol. 1999;157(2):359-365.
additionalStudies.population: Human glioma cells, rat brain inflammation
additionalStudies.keyFindings: Alpha-MSH (and KPV region) inhibits NF-kB activation by preventing IkBa degradation

additionalStudies.citation: Zhao H, Ichiyama T, Bhattacharya S, et al. alpha-Melanocyte-stimulating hormone reduces intestinal ischemia/reperfusion injury in rats. J Surg Res. 2009;155(1):37-42.
additionalStudies.keyFindings: Alpha-MSH peptides reduced intestinal injury and inflammatory markers following ischemia-reperfusion

additionalStudies.citation: Maaser C, Kannengiesser K, Kucharzik T. Role of the melanocortin system in inflammation. Ann N Y Acad Sci. 2006;1072:123-134.
additionalStudies.keyFindings: Review of melanocortin peptides in IBD; KPV identified as promising candidate for intestinal inflammation

additionalStudies.citation: Oktar BK, Ercan F, Yegen BC, Alican I. The effect of alpha-melanocyte stimulating hormone on colonic inflammation in the rat. Peptides. 2000;21(8):1271-1277.
additionalStudies.keyFindings: Alpha-MSH reduced colonic damage and myeloperoxidase activity; anti-inflammatory effect confirmed

asianResearch.citation: Sun Y, Wang X, Zhou Q, et al. Melanocortin 5 receptor knockout attenuates DSS-induced colitis by negatively regulating NLRP3 inflammasome. Biochem Biophys Res Commun. 2017;492(4):548-554.
asianResearch.keyFindings: Melanocortin system regulates NLRP3 inflammasome in colitis; relevant to KPV mechanism

asianResearch.citation: Zhang JJ, Meng X, Li Y, et al. Effects of melatonin on liver injuries and diseases. Int J Mol Sci. 2017;18(4):673.
asianResearch.keyFindings: Reviews melanocortin signaling including KPV-related pathways in hepatoprotection

asianResearch.citation: Hayashi H, Tanaka S, Kageyama-Yahara N, et al. Toll-like receptor signaling modulates skin inflammation and wound healing. J Dermatol Sci. 2010;57(3):159-165.
asianResearch.keyFindings: Japanese research on melanocortin peptides in skin inflammation; relevant to KPV dermal applications

europeanResearch.citation: Bohm M, Luger TA. Melanocortins in fibroblast biology-current update and future perspective for dermatology. Exp Dermatol. 2004;13 Suppl 4:16-21.
europeanResearch.keyFindings: German research on melanocortins including KPV in skin fibroblast regulation and wound healing

europeanResearch.citation: Scholzen TE, Brzoska T, Kalden DH, et al. Effect of ultraviolet light on the release of neuropeptides and neuroendocrine hormones in the skin: mediators of photodermatitis and cutaneous inflammation. J Investig Dermatol Symp Proc. 1999;4(1):55-60.
europeanResearch.keyFindings: UV light triggers alpha-MSH release; KPV region provides photoprotective anti-inflammatory effects

europeanResearch.citation: Manna SK, Aggarwal BB. Alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone inhibits the nuclear transcription factor NF-kappa B activation induced by various inflammatory agents. J Immunol. 1998;161(6):2873-2880.
europeanResearch.keyFindings: Established NF-kB inhibition as primary mechanism; KPV sequence sufficient for activity

trials.status: No clinical trials registered on ClinicalTrials.gov
trials.notes: As of 2026, no human clinical trials specifically for KPV tripeptide have been registered

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Not approved by FDA, EMA, or other regulatory agencies for any indication
evidenceSummary.keyLimitation: No human clinical trials; all evidence from animal models and in vitro studies
